record.field: Sales, CRM, and customer management tools

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to find, look up, search for, or get information about existing customers, suppliers, vendors, or contacts. Trigger phrases: "find customer", "search for partner", "lookup client", "show me [name]", "who is", "contact details", "customer info". Do NOT use this tool to create new partners (use partner_create) or to view specific record details by ID (use partner_details). Returns: list of matching partners with ID, name, email, phone, and address.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to add, create, register, or create a new customer, supplier, vendor, or contact in the system. Trigger phrases: "create customer", "add partner", "new client", "register contact", "add to database", "make a new [customer/supplier]". Do NOT use this tool to search for existing records (use partner_search) or to view details (use partner_details). Requires user confirmation before execution. Returns: ID of the newly created partner.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to find, look up, or search for existing sales orders or quotes. Trigger phrases: "find order", "search sales", "show orders", "list quotes", "order status", "pending orders", "draft orders", "recent sales". Do NOT use this tool to create new orders (use sale_order_create) or to confirm an order (use sale_order_confirm). Do NOT use for invoices - use invoice_search instead. Returns: list of orders with ID, customer, amount, date, and state.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to create, generate, make, or prepare a new sales order, quote, or sales quotation for a customer. Trigger phrases: "create order", "new order", "make a sale", "generate quote", "prepare quotation", "add to order". Do NOT use this to search orders (use sale_order_search) or to confirm orders (use sale_order_confirm). Requires user confirmation before execution. Returns: ID of the created order.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to confirm, validate, approve, or lock a draft sales order or quotation - turning it into a confirmed sale. Trigger phrases: "confirm order", "approve quote", "validate sale", "lock order", "confirm sale", "make order official". Do NOT use this to create orders (use sale_order_create) or search orders (use sale_order_search). Requires user confirmation. Returns: success confirmation with order ID.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to find, search, look up, or list sales leads, opportunities, or prospects in the CRM pipeline. Trigger phrases: "find lead", "search opportunities", "show prospects", "pipeline", "lead status", "sales pipeline", "new leads", "pending opportunities". Do NOT use this to create leads (use crm_lead_create) or to manage confirmed customers (use partner_search). Returns: list of leads with name, company, stage, probability, and expected revenue.

[SALES & CRM] Use this tool when the user wants to create, add, register, or generate a new sales lead, opportunity, or prospect in the CRM system. Trigger phrases: "create lead", "new opportunity", "add prospect", "register lead", "new sales lead", "create opportunity". Do NOT use this to search leads (use crm_lead_search) or to convert to customer (use partner_create). Requires user confirmation. Returns: ID of the created lead.

[FINANCE] Use this tool when the user wants to find, search, look up, or list invoices, bills, or payment requests. Trigger phrases: "find invoice", "search invoice", "show invoices", "list bills", "invoice status", "pending payment", "outstanding invoices", "paid invoices", "unpaid invoices". Do NOT use this for sales orders (use sale_order_search) or to create invoices (use invoice_create - if available). Returns: list of invoices with ID, number, customer, amount, date, and state.

[FINANCE] Use this tool when the user wants to view, see, get, or check the full details of a specific invoice including line items, taxes, and payment information. Trigger phrases: "invoice details", "show invoice", "invoice lines", "invoice items", "get invoice", "view invoice [ID]", "full invoice". Do NOT use this to search for invoices (use invoice_search). Requires knowing the specific invoice ID - find it first using invoice_search. Returns: full invoice details with line items, taxes, and payment info.

[INVENTORY & OPERATIONS] Use this tool when the user wants to find, search, look up, or list products, items, or goods in the system. Trigger phrases: "find product", "search item", "show products", "product list", "catalog", "inventory", "SKU", "what products", "available items", "pricing". Do NOT use this to check stock levels (use stock_quantity_search) or to create products. Returns: list of products with ID, name, SKU, price, and available quantity.

[INVENTORY & OPERATIONS] Use this tool when the user wants to check, view, see, get, or find inventory levels, stock quantities, available items, or warehouse quantities. Trigger phrases: "check stock", "inventory level", "available quantity", "in stock", "stock level", "how many", "warehouse stock", "product availability", "on hand". Do NOT use this to search for products (use product_search). Returns: product, warehouse location, available quantity, and reserved quantity.

[OPERATIONS & HR] Use this tool when the user wants to create, schedule, add, or set up a meeting, event, appointment, or calendar entry. Trigger phrases: "create meeting", "schedule meeting", "add event", "book appointment", "set up call", "calendar invite", "schedule demo", "book time". Do NOT use this for sending emails or messages. Requires user confirmation. Returns: ID of the created event.

[HR & OPERATIONS] Use this tool when the user wants to create, open, log, or register a support ticket, helpdesk issue, customer support request, or service ticket. Trigger phrases: "create ticket", "open support ticket", "log issue", "raise support request", "report problem", "helpdesk", "support case", "report bug". Do NOT use this for general tasks (use calendar_event_create) or to search tickets (use helpdesk_ticket_search if available). Requires user confirmation. Returns: ID of the created ticket.

[HR & OPERATIONS] Use this tool when the user wants to find, search, look up, or list employees, staff members, or team members in the company. Trigger phrases: "find employee", "search staff", "show employees", "who is", "staff directory", "team list", "employee contact", "HR lookup", "colleague". Do NOT use this to search customers (use partner_search) or to create employees (use hr_employee_create if available). Returns: list of employees with name, email, department, job title, and phone.
